operation.semantics: Add participants to an existing calendar event.
Append content to an existing file.
Cancel a calendar event by id.
Create a calendar event and notify the participant list. `start_time` and `end_time` must use YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM.
Delete an existing email by id.
Delete a cloud file by id.
Reschedule an existing calendar event. `new_start_time` and `new_end_time` must use YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM.
Send an email message to one or more recipients.
Share a file with one recipient and a permission level.

semantics.data: Load all calendar events for one day. `day` must use YYYY-MM-DD.
semantics.text: Load all calendar events for one day. `day` must use YYYY-MM-DD.

semantics.data: List cloud files. Returned file_entry records include the content field when available, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.
semantics.text: List cloud files. Returned file_entry records include the content field when available, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.

semantics.data: Search calendar events by query and optional date filter. If `date` is provided, it must use YYYY-MM-DD.
semantics.text: Search calendar events by query and optional date filter. If `date` is provided, it must use YYYY-MM-DD.

semantics.data: Search email by query string and optional sender filter. Returned email_msg records already include the body field, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.
semantics.text: Search email by query string and optional sender filter. Returned email_msg records already include the body field, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.

semantics.data: Search email content without pinning the sender. Returned email_msg records already include the body field, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.
semantics.text: Search email content without pinning the sender. Returned email_msg records already include the body field, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.

semantics.data: Search cloud files by free-text query. Returned file_entry records include the content field when available, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.
semantics.text: Search cloud files by free-text query. Returned file_entry records include the content field when available, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.

semantics.data: Search cloud files by filename. Returned file_entry records include the content field when available, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.
semantics.text: Search cloud files by filename. Returned file_entry records include the content field when available, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.
